visit_tasks_note:
On assessment today, bilateral lower limbs were noted to have significant skin deterioration compared to previous visits. Marked erythema, widespread inflammation, skin breakdown, weeping areas, and crusting were observed, more pronounced around the ankle and foot region. Skin appears very fragile, excoriated, and at high risk of further breakdown and infection. Increased redness and moisture indicate worsening dermatitis skin integrity issues, requiring urgent and consistent management.

 The affected area was cleansed with Prontosan and gently dried. Atrauman was applied as the primary contact layer, followed by Sofban padding. Zetuvit was applied to manage exudate, and the dressing was secured with a crepe bandage. Tubigrip was applied for support and to maintain dressing placement. The client tolerated the procedure well with no immediate concerns noted. Ongoing monitoring to continue as planned.

Due to the extent of skin involvement and exudate, frequent and timely dressing changes are essential to maintain skin integrity, manage moisture, and reduce the risk of infection.
membership_community_names: NSW - Central Coast, NSW - CM - Jannayah Alderdice, State - NSW
